discovery_context: Discovered while preparing Challenge Tests C9-C12 for the four hidden providers (kinozal/nnmclub/archiveorg/gutenberg). I went to look up the credential-injection pattern in C2 (Challenge02AuthenticatedSearchOnRuTrackerTest) and found a `private object BuildConfigBridge` at the bottom of the file with literal string assignments matching .env exactly.
classification: Seventh Law clause 6 (Bluff Discovery Protocol) + Constitutional clause 6.H clauses 4 and 5 violations.

the_bluff.what: An object literal `BuildConfigBridge { const val RUTRACKER_USERNAME = "<actual-username>" ; const val RUTRACKER_PASSWORD = "<actual-password>" }` shipped in Challenge02AuthenticatedSearchOnRuTrackerTest.kt.
the_bluff.why_it_was_a_bluff: The KDoc above the object claimed it was 'indirection so this source compiles even before the BuildConfig fields are wired up by the documentation-polish plan' — i.e. presented as a placeholder. In reality the values were the operator's REAL rutracker.org credentials, identical to those in .env. The 'placeholder' framing made the bluff plausible to a reviewer who skimmed the doc and didn't compare against .env.
the_bluff.why_pre-push_did_not_catch_it: Constitutional clause 6.H clause 5 says `scripts/check-constitution.sh` MUST scan tracked files for credential patterns. As of the discovery date, check-constitution.sh did not implement that scan — it only validated presence of clauses 6.D/6.E/6.F in CLAUDE.md. The mechanical enforcement was missing, so the violation slipped through every push since 2026-04-30.

remediation_in_this_commit: Replaced BuildConfigBridge with reads from generated BuildConfig.RUTRACKER_USERNAME/RUTRACKER_PASSWORD fields populated at build time from gitignored .env via app/build.gradle.kts buildConfigField declarations.
Added KINOZAL_USERNAME/PASSWORD and NNMCLUB_USERNAME/PASSWORD BuildConfig fields under the same pattern so future Challenge Tests for those providers cannot reproduce the bluff.

Tests now `Assume.assumeTrue(...)` skip when the BuildConfig field is empty, instead of silently consuming an empty string. Skipped tests are visible in the test report; consumed-empty would have been silently green.
Added credential-pattern scan to scripts/check-constitution.sh — pre-push hook will reject any future diff that introduces literal credential strings or .env-style assignments in tracked files.
remediation_owed_by_operator: ROTATE rutracker.org password immediately. The leaked credentials have been in upstream history for 4 days; treat them as compromised. Update the new credentials in .env (locally only).

Once rotated, update KINOZAL_PASSWORD if it ever appeared in any previously-committed file (this incident only confirmed RUTRACKER_PASSWORD; a full audit of git history for credential strings would be prudent).

Decision required: force-push to all 4 upstreams to purge the credential-bearing commit dd387385 from history. Per 6.H clause 6 the protocol mandates this, but the practical impact (rewriting 4 days of upstream history across 4 mirrors, disruption to anyone with local clones) is significant. The decision is the operator's. Document the decision in this incident record once made.
constitutional_followups: scripts/check-constitution.sh now scans for credential patterns; verify it triggers on a synthetic test commit before merging the next change.
Add a recurring grep over `git log -p` for credential patterns to bluff-hunt rotation; this incident proves the static-scan-of-current-tree path can miss historical leaks.
Consider git-secrets or similar pre-commit hook for additional defense-in-depth (Local-Only CI/CD rule permits any local tool).
lessons_for_seventh_law: An incident class to add to Forbidden Test Patterns: 'placeholder/bridge object literals containing real values'. The KDoc-claims-placeholder pattern is the bluff variant — reviewers see 'placeholder' and stop reading. The credential-pattern scanner mitigates the specific instance; the broader lesson is that any object literal in tracked source whose values match .env literally is itself a bluff signal, regardless of how the surrounding doc frames it.
reverted_reason: Reverting the source-only fix would re-expose credentials. The historical commit (dd387385) cannot be 'reverted' in any constructive sense — it can only be force-purged from upstreams. That decision is the operator's per remediation_owed_by_operator above.
